strengths: Current hands-on experience building AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows — directly aligned with the role's agentic-AI mandate

Deep distributed-systems and real-time backend expertise: low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, async I/O, event-driven architecture (GISCollective), and observability-minded craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)

Relevant fintech and pricing-systems exposure: reshaped investment infrastructure at RIDE Capital and built multi-country pricing optimization at Optilyz, plus ~19 years of engineering experience across the full stack
concerns: No demonstrated executive or engineering-management experience — resume shows senior IC and founder roles, not scaling teams from 10 to 30+ or developing a technical-leadership layer
Agentic AI work appears recent and tooling-focused rather than operating production multi-agent systems with financial consequences in regulated environments
No explicit evidence of LLM selection, fine-tuning, or evaluation-methodology depth, nor of governing hybrid/outsourced engineering models

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and real-time backend depth

response.justification: This candidate offers a rare combination the role's technical core demands: active, hands-on agentic-AI infrastructure work (MCP tooling, agent frameworks) layered on top of genuinely hard distributed-systems experience — millions of requests per second, low-latency real-time data, event-driven platform design — plus meaningful fintech exposure at RIDE Capital and pricing-engine work at Optilyz. His founder experience running GISCollective for nearly a decade demonstrates product ownership, architectural judgment, and long-horizon system stewardship, while his record of teaching, workshops, and craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD) maps well to the role's emphasis on evaluation rigor and engineering culture. For a scale-up seeking a technically credible leader who can personally architect the multi-agent platform rather than manage from a distance, he is a strong technical fit whose leadership scope, rather than engineering depth, is the primary open question.
